title: Team Onboarding
text: Set up one shared base where each member can own one prototype module while keeping style and structure consistent.

title: Team Onboarding
text: Set up one shared base where each member can own one prototype module while keeping style and structure consistent.

title: Team Onboarding
text:
Suggested Team Split

Member A: EDA + confirmatory module.
Member B: cluster module.
Member C: decision tree module.
Member D (optional): integration, QA, and deployment.

title: Team Onboarding
text:
Branching Convention

Create branches as codex/<module-name> or feature/<module-name>.
Keep pull requests scoped to one module/page at a time.
Merge only after quarto render and app smoke-test pass.

title: Team Onboarding
text:
Local Setup
git clone <repo-url>
cd group_project
quarto preview
Rscript run_app.R 3838

title: Team Onboarding
text:

_quarto.yml (navigation and build behavior)
styles/theme.scss (global styling)
app/app.R (module registration)

text:
flowchart LR

title: UI Storyboard
text:
UI Layout Plan

Left Panel (Controls)

Period Filter (checkboxes)
K (Cluster Count) (slider)
Random Seed (numeric input)
Scale Features (toggle)
Run Clustering (button)



Right Panel (Outputs)

Silhouette Score (KPI card)
Cluster Scatter Plot (main chart)
Cluster Profile Table (interactive table)
Cluster Assignment Preview (download-ready table)

title: UI Storyboard
text:
UI Component Mapping



UI Need
Shiny Component
Reason




Multi-period comparison
checkboxGroupInput
flexible grouping


Limited integer tuning
sliderInput
fast constrained control


Reproducibility
numericInput
explicit seed control


Trigger heavy compute
actionButton
avoid unnecessary reruns


Fast profile scan
DT::datatable
sortable/filterable

title: UI Storyboard
text:
Usability Notes

Keep defaults aligned with assignment baseline (k = 3).
Keep labels domain-driven (pandemic shock, recovery, high-performance).
Preserve color semantics across plot and table.

title: Module Prototype: Cluster Analysis
text: Prototype a Shiny module that groups monthly observations into interpretable tourism market states.

title: Module Prototype: Cluster Analysis
text: Prototype a Shiny module that groups monthly observations into interpretable tourism market states.

title: Module Prototype: Cluster Analysis
text:
Data Contract
Input dataset: data/raw/tourism_four_part_analysis_ready.xlsx
Sheet: analysis_ready_all
Required fields:

visitor_arrivals
china_share
hotel_occ
avg_stay_monthly_capped
period
date

title: Module Prototype: Cluster Analysis
text:
Analytical Design

Filter data by selected period.
Select feature set.
Optionally standardize features.
Run k-means with user-defined k.
Compute silhouette score.
Return cluster assignments, profile summary, and scatter plot.

title: Module Prototype: Cluster Analysis
text:
Parameter Exposure









Parameter
UI Component
Default
Validation




period_filter
checkboxGroupInput
all periods
at least one selected


k_value
sliderInput
3
min 2, max 6


random_seed
numericInput
42
integer > 0


scale_features
checkboxInput
TRUE
boolean

text:
Output Exposure



Output
Format
Purpose




Scatter plot
ggplot2
Show market states visually


Profile table
DT::datatable
Compare cluster means


Silhouette metric
text value
Indicate clustering quality

title: Module Prototype: Cluster Analysis
text:
Implementation Files

app/R/data_utils.R
app/R/mod_cluster_ui.R
app/R/mod_cluster_server.R
app/app.R

title: Module Prototype: Cluster Analysis
text:
Quality Gates

No NA rows in selected features before clustering.
Error message if filtered sample is too small for chosen k.
Stable color palette across outputs.
Reproducible clustering with fixed seed.

title: Shiny App Guide
text:
The app follows a modular structure:

app/app.R - top-level layout and module wiring.
app/R/data_utils.R - dataset loading and preprocessing helpers.
app/R/mod_cluster_ui.R - cluster module UI.
app/R/mod_cluster_server.R - cluster module logic.

title: Shiny App Guide
text:
The app follows a modular structure:

app/app.R - top-level layout and module wiring.
app/R/data_utils.R - dataset loading and preprocessing helpers.
app/R/mod_cluster_ui.R - cluster module UI.
app/R/mod_cluster_server.R - cluster module logic.

text:

Rscript run_app.R 3838

title: Project Home

Why This Structure
The information architecture is adapted from the strengths of these references:

Habari Tanzania: one-page storytelling with section anchors and team visibility.
Decoding Chaos: Quarto-native multi-page prototype navigation.

title: Project Home
text:
Workstreams


A. Submission-Ready Report
take-home-ex2.qmd is the individual submission page in Quarto HTML format.


B. Prototype Module
prototype/module-cluster.qmd defines module logic, parameters, UI, and output behavior.


C. Running Shiny App
app/app.R with modular server code in app/R/.


D. Shared Team Base
team/onboarding.qmd documents setup, roles, and branch workflow.

Interpretation

Packages marked cran_available = TRUE are available from CRAN.
Packages marked installed = TRUE are already ready on this machine.
Packages not yet installed can be added via install.packages().

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
This page is structured as an individual Take-home Exercise 2 submission and focuses on one prototype module:
Selected module: Cluster Analysis for monthly tourism market states.
The module uses monthly observations to identify interpretable tourism states:

Pandemic shock months.
Recovery transition months.
High-performance months.

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
This page is structured as an individual Take-home Exercise 2 submission and focuses on one prototype module:
Selected module: Cluster Analysis for monthly tourism market states.
The module uses monthly observations to identify interpretable tourism states:

Pandemic shock months.
Recovery transition months.
High-performance months.

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
1. Data Preparation Process
Data preparation follows the imported bilingual plan and detailed cleaning notes in data/references/.
Key preparation decisions:

Remove metadata rows and invalid 2015-12-01 record.
Use monthly variables for model features.
Keep annual variables only for supplementary visual context.
Create china_share, period, and calendar fields (year, month, quarter).
Use capped stay-length and z-score fields for stable clustering.

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
2. Package Support (CRAN)
The module package audit is documented in Package Audit.
Core stack for this prototype:



Package
Purpose
CRAN




shiny
UI/server runtime
Yes


readxl
Load .xlsx data
Yes


dplyr
Data wrangling
Yes


ggplot2
Visual outputs
Yes


cluster
Silhouette scoring
Yes


DT
Interactive result table
Yes

title: Take-home Exercise 2
section: 3. Prototype Code Test and Expected Visual Output
text:
3. Prototype Code Test and Expected Visual Output
Smoke-test script: scripts/prototype_smoke_test.R
Generated outputs:

Elbow curve for candidate K.
Cluster-state scatter visualization.
Cluster profile table.




Elbow Plot





Cluster Scatter

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
4. Exposed Parameters and Outputs



Type
Item
Description




Input
period_filter
Filter period: pre-COVID, shock, recovery


Input
k_value
Number of clusters (2-6)


Input
scale_features
Enable/disable z-score standardization


Input
random_seed
Reproducible k-means assignment


Output
Cluster scatter plot
2D visual state map


Output
Cluster profile table
Mean values by cluster


Output
Silhouette score
Quick quality indicator

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
5. UI Design
UI design principles in this prototype:

Progressive disclosure: show defaults first, advanced controls optional.
Interpretability-first: output labels use domain terms, not only model terms.
Comparability: consistent color mapping between plot and table.

Detailed storyboard and component mapping are provided at UI Storyboard.

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
6. Visual Design & Interactivity Practices
This prototype applies:

Clear visual hierarchy for decision steps.
Explicit mapping between controls and outputs.
Responsive layout for laptop and mobile viewport.
Data-informed defaults to reduce trial-and-error.

title: Take-home Exercise 2
text:
7. Current Status and Next Step
Completed:

Modular app skeleton.
Data integration and smoke-test artifacts.
Quarto write-up scaffold aligned to assignment requirements.

Next:

Add confirmatory stats module.
Add decision-tree module with feature importance panel.
Add deployment pipeline for GitHub Pages + hosted Shiny endpoint.
